exemplar.title: Dog therapy; A Happier Way Out of Hard Times

detailedExemplarData.title: Dog therapy; A Happier Way Out of Hard Times

strengths: Thoughtful justification of primary vs. secondary research showing effective research skill application
Interview questions reflect strong critical thinking and planning ATL skills
Screenshot evidence of editing software use clearly illustrates technical skill development
weaknesses: Lacks explicit references to communication ATL skills when describing professional contacts
Self-management strategies (e.g., managing nerves before interviews) are not fully detailed
Connections between listed ATL skills and concrete examples need strengthening
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You provide **solid evidence** of your research and technical ATL skills, particularly in planning and critical thinking. To enhance this section, explicitly name and exemplify your communication and self-management strategies, and tie each ATL skill to a specific action or outcome.

strengths: Strong, personally relevant learning goal linking dog therapy to prior experiences
Thoughtful decision-making in refocusing from animal art therapy to human dog therapy
Detailed sequencing of long-term goals into weekly tasks enhancing project feasibility
weaknesses: Success criteria lack quantifiable targets (e.g., number of interviews, view counts)
Incomplete entries and abrupt endings in the action plan table
Inconsistent terminology in criterion labels detracts from clarity and rubric alignment
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates **clear purpose** and a well-justified shift in focus, supported by a coherent timeline of weekly tasks. To strengthen this section, develop **measurable success criteria**, ensure every action-plan cell is fully populated, and standardize your rubric terminology for precision and consistency.

strengths: Deep ethical reflection on empathy and confidentiality in professional contexts
Insightful recognition of personal growth in structuring thoughts and professional confidence
Balanced self-assessment that acknowledges both successes and technical areas for improvement
weaknesses: Evaluations against success criteria lack specific evidence (e.g., viewer feedback data, timing logs)
Reflections are not consistently linked back to each success criterion
Future goals are stated but lack concrete next steps, and time-management strategies for procrastination are not specified
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflecting shows **considerable depth**, especially in ethical insight and self-awareness. To push this further, anchor your evaluations with **concrete data** against each success criterion, link reflections directly to those criteria, and outline actionable strategies for future improvement and time management.

annotations.comment: Your table outlines key criteria but lacks quantifiable measures (for instance, number of interviews conducted or view count goals). Adding specific metrics makes evaluation more objective.
You establish a strong learning goal by linking personal experience with a clear focus on dog therapy. This personal narrative creates meaningful context and motivation for your project.
Consider refining your topic statement with specific, measurable outcomes (e.g., number of case studies analyzed or test subjects) to make your learning goal more evaluative.
The success criteria section ends abruptly. Ensure each criterion is fully described and includes measurable targets (e.g., video length confirmation, audience engagement metrics).
Your explanation of shifting from art therapy for animals to dog therapy for humans shows clear reflection and justified decision-making, demonstrating a thoughtful planning process.
Breaking your long-term goals into weekly tasks demonstrates effective sequencing and resource planning, which enhances the feasibility of your project timeline.
Several cells in your action plan table are empty. Complete all entries with specific activities, deadlines, and responsible parties to ensure a coherent, detailed plan.
Criterion labels vary (e.g., âCriteria A: planningâ, âCriteria B; researchâ)âstandardize terminology to align with IB rubric strands for clarity and consistency.
You effectively justify the balance between primary and secondary research, demonstrating thoughtful application of research ATL skills.
When describing contact with professionals, explicitly reference communication skills (e.g., drafting emails, active listening) to strengthen your evidence of ATL skill application.
Your psychiatrist interview questions show critical thinking and planning, indicating strong use of research and thinking ATL skills.
You note initial nervousness before the interviewâconsider describing specific self-management strategies you used to prepare and build confidence.
Including a screenshot of your editing software provides clear evidence of technical skill application and enhances credibility.
You list self-management and research skills developed; strengthen this by linking each to a concrete example (e.g., scheduling interviews, organizing notes).
Your Affective skills section insightfully reflects on empathy, ethics, and professional confidentialityâdemonstrating deep personal and ethical growth.
When evaluating against success criteria, include specific evidence (e.g., viewer feedback summaries, timing logs) to substantiate claims of meeting each criterion.
Reflecting on confidentiality during the Dr. Tirza meeting reveals a sophisticated understanding of ethics and respect for participant privacy.
Your Reflection skills section is insightful but would benefit from directly linking reflections back to each success criterion to show thorough evaluation.
You articulate personal growth in structuring thoughts and professional confidence, showing strong reflective depth on your learning journey.
Your overall evaluation candidly recognizes both achievements and areas for technical improvement, showing balanced self-assessment.
Your acknowledgment of procrastination is honest; extend this by describing strategies you applied to improve time management for future projects.
Your learner profile reflections are thoughtful; strengthen them by explicitly linking attributes (e.g., open-mindedness, caring) to specific ATL skills you applied.
Your future goals link well to psychology studies; enhance this by outlining concrete next steps or courses to build on your project learnings.
